title: atom charset restriction (was jssm#754)
manager: Today a state can be named `foo,bar#baz`, which breaks tooling, highlighting, and the coming expression language (where dot is member access and dash is the arrow). 6.0 restricts unquoted names to identifier form; anything more exotic must be quoted. This is a breaking change, so it rides the 6.0 wall with a corpus sweep and a migration note — it lands once, loudly, with a quote-it suggestion, instead of trickling out and surprising people.
cs: peg `AtomFirstLetter/AtomLetter` + enum-member rule -> `[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*`; example/test corpus sweep; diagnostic carries span + suggested_fix (quoted form); vectors accept identifiers and reject `a.b`/`1a`/`a-b` with correct messages; manifest entry already written.
